info.name: CNPM Backend API
info.description:
Postman collection for the CNPM backend mounted at `/api/v1`. Modules: health-check, auth, user/users, address/addresses, cart, category/categories, delivery/deliveries, discount/discounts, image, inventory, order/orders, product/products, role/roles, shop/shops, warehouse/warehouses.

Conventions:
- Authentication: bearer access token by default (collection-level). Routes that need the refresh token override the bearer at the request level (`Logout`, `Refresh Token`). Public/`access-optional` routes still send the access bearer; clear `{{accessToken}}` to test anonymous behavior.
- The `Login` and `Refresh Token` requests automatically capture `accessToken`/`refreshToken` from the response and store them as collection variables.
- JWT-scoped identity: request bodies never carry `userId`, `shopId`, or `ownerId`. Those values are injected by the controller from the JWT payload. The only exception is `PATCH /shop/status` which accepts a shop `id` (admin-only).
- Idempotency: `POST /cart/items`, `POST /order/checkout`, and `POST /order/:id/cancel` honor an `Idempotency-Key` header (required for `POST /order/checkout`). The header value must match `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{8,128}`.
- Pagination defaults: `page=1`, `limit=30`, `sort=desc`. Shared `limit` accepts `5..50` (see `src/shared/schema.ts`). Each module's `orderBy` accepts whitelisted values defined in its `*.constants.ts`.

item.name: User
item.description: Singleton current-user routes (`src/api/user/user.router.ts`) and list route (`src/api/user/users.router.ts`). The current user is inferred from the access token's `userId` — the request body never carries `userId`.

item.name: Address
item.description: Personal and shop addresses (`src/api/address/address.router.ts`, `src/api/address/addresses.router.ts`). The owning `userId`/`shopId` is derived from the access token JWT, so request bodies never include them.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Home",
  "addressLine": "123 Main St",
  "city": "Ho Chi Minh",
  "district": "District 1",
  "state": "Ho Chi Minh",
  "country": "Vietnam",
  "latitude": "10.7626",
  "longitude": "106.6602",
  "isPrimary": true
}

{
  "name": "Warehouse HQ",
  "addressLine": "99 Industrial Park",
  "city": "Ho Chi Minh",
  "district": "District 7",
  "state": "Ho Chi Minh",
  "country": "Vietnam",
  "isPrimary": true
}

item.name: Cart
item.description: Authenticated cart routes (`src/api/cart/cart.router.ts`). The cart belongs to the JWT user. `POST /cart/items` supports an optional `Idempotency-Key` header to deduplicate retried adds.

item.name: Category
item.description: Category management (`src/api/category/category.router.ts`) and global listing (`src/api/category/categories.router.ts`). Read endpoints accept anonymous requests via `access-optional`. Write/tree endpoints are rate-limited to 20 requests/min per route.

item.name: Delivery
item.description: Shipping module (`src/api/delivery/delivery.router.ts`, `src/api/delivery/deliveries.router.ts`). Methods/zones/rates are configuration entities; deliveries are the runtime shipments attached to orders.

item.name: Discount
item.description: Discount module (`src/api/discount/discount.router.ts`, `src/api/discount/discounts.router.ts`). Shop-scoped routes (`/discount/shop/...`) derive `shopId` from the JWT; the global `POST /discount/` is admin-only and accepts an explicit `shopId` when `scope = shop`. `valueType` ∈ `fixed | percentage`; `discountType` ∈ `items | delivery`; `scope` ∈ `global | shop`. Rules: `min_subtotal` requires `{ amount }`, `min_item_count` requires `{ count }`.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Free Shipping (Global)",
  "code": "FREESHIP",
  "description": "Waives delivery fee on qualifying orders",
  "value": "100",
  "valueType": "percentage",
  "discountType": "delivery",
  "scope": "global",
  "maxUses": 5000,
  "isActive": true,
  "rules": [
    { "type": "min_subtotal", "params": { "amount": "500000.00" } }
  ]
}

request.description: When `scope = shop`, also provide `shopId` and `discountType = items`. `targetSpuIds` is only accepted for shop-scope items discounts.

item.name: Image
item.description: Presigned-URL provisioning (`src/api/image/image.router.ts`). Allowed prefixes: `user-avatar`, `product-image`. Allowed extensions: `jpg | jpeg | png`. Max sizes: 2MB (avatar), 5MB (product).

item.name: Inventory
item.description: Inventory adjustments (`src/api/inventory/inventory.router.ts`). Quantity mutations use either a hard `set` (`PATCH /:skuId/:warehouseId`) or a delta `adjust` (`POST /:skuId/:warehouseId/adjust`). `expectedVersion` enables optimistic concurrency control.

item.name: Order
item.description: Checkout, placement, and lifecycle (`src/api/order/order.router.ts`, `src/api/order/orders.router.ts`). `POST /order/checkout` **requires** `Idempotency-Key`. `POST /order/:id/cancel` accepts an optional `Idempotency-Key`.

request.description: `status` ∈ pending | confirmed | processing | shipped | delivered | completed. Use `POST /order/:id/cancel` to cancel.

query.description: pending | confirmed | processing | shipped | delivered | completed | cancelled

item.name: Product
item.description:
Product CRUD plus SKU/attribute composition (`src/api/product/product.router.ts`) and search (`src/api/product/products.router.ts`). Prices use string-decimal format (e.g. `"49.99"`).

Notes:
- `GET /products/` is `access-optional` — anonymous callers are allowed; clear `{{accessToken}}` to test guest behavior.
- `categoryId` on `GET /products/` expands to the category subtree (any descendant matches via `category_closure`).
- `GET /product/:idOrSlug` accepts either a product UUID or its slug; the `{{productId}}` variable holds whichever value you captured.
- Product detail responses include the joined `category` summary (id, name, slug, parentId, iconUrl) and a per-SKU `quantity` (available stock summed across warehouses, `quantity - reserved_quantity`).

query.description: Expands to the full subtree via category_closure

request.description: Auth: `access-optional` — clear `{{accessToken}}` to test anonymous access. `categoryId` matches the category and all of its descendants.
Path param is `:idOrSlug` — accepts either a product UUID or its slug. Response includes the joined `category` summary and a `quantity` field on each SKU (available stock summed across warehouses).
Returns every permission row in the DB (system-wide catalog). Requires `ROLE_READ`. Moved from `/roles/permissions` — that URL now returns the caller's own role+permissions.
Returns the caller's role + permissions, resolved from `jwtPayload.roleId`. No RBAC — available to any authenticated user. Useful for the client to know what the current session can do.
Same response as `GET /role/` (caller's role + permissions) but exposed under the plural collection and gated by `ROLE_READ`. Provided for role-management UIs that already hold `ROLE_READ`.
Fetch a role by id (with permissions). Caller needs any of `ROLE_READ`, `ROLE_UPDATE`, or `ROLE_DELETE` — the update/delete flows pre-load the role from the same endpoint.

item.name: Shop
item.description: Shop lifecycle (`src/api/shop/shop.router.ts`, `src/api/shop/shops.router.ts`). `POST /shop/` grants `shop_owner` to the caller (one owner per shop). `POST /shop/workers` is performed by an owner to assign an existing user to their shop. `PATCH /shop/status` is the **only** shop endpoint that accepts an explicit `id` because it is admin-only.

item.name: Get Shop Details (by id or slug)
Warehouse
item.description: Warehouse management (`src/api/warehouse/warehouse.router.ts`, `src/api/warehouse/warehouses.router.ts`). `addressId` must reference an address that the requesting shop owns.
